entities.name: IDENTIFICATION AND ENUMERATION METHOD
entities.description: A novel method for counting total culturable and antagonistic microorganisms in a source such as compost

entities.name: MICROORGANISMS
entities.description: Biological entities found in compost, including bacteria and fungi, some of which are antagonistic to pathogens

entities.name: COMPOST
entities.description: The organic source material from which microorganisms are isolated and enumerated

entities.name: FUSARIUM SOLANI
entities.description: A specific fungal plant pathogen used as a test fungus to identify antagonistic microorganisms

entities.name: POTATO DEXTROSE AGAR
entities.description: A growth medium used in the enumeration method, specifically in quarter-strength

entities.name: LAMINAR FLOW
entities.description: A process used to expose the surface of the agar for 4 hours during the enumeration method

entities.name: TOTAL CULTURABLE ORGANISMS
entities.description: Microorganisms from a sample that are capable of growing on the provided culture medium

entities.name: ANTAGONISTIC MICROORGANISMS
entities.description: Microorganisms that inhibit the growth of fungal plant pathogens, identified by a halo around their colonies

entities.name: BACTERIA
entities.description: A type of microorganism found in Biodynamic preparations and evaluated for antagonistic properties

entities.name: BIODYNAMIC PREPARATIONS
entities.description: Specific compost samples used by organic farmers, used as sources for bacterial isolation

entities.name: PLANT PATHOGENIC FUNGI
entities.description: Fungi that cause diseases in plants, used as targets for suppression by antagonistic microorganisms

entities.name: DUAL CULTURE METHOD
entities.description: A method used to evaluate the ability of bacterial isolates to suppress plant pathogenic fungi
